title: Hypothetical Question FAR Christian Angel Hierarchy
display_title: Hypothetical Question FAR Christian Angel Hierarchy
wikitext:
[]: Hypothetical Question: FAR = Christian Angel Hierarchy             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Wed Aug 13, 2008 11:58 pm Read this: [url]http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Christian_angelic_hierarchy[/url] Could one say that the FAR could, hypothetically, be a play on the hierarchy of angels? Could God be the top FAR and have created the "giants" (Adam and Lilith) to seed the universe? Could the angels from the show be in the "Angels" rank in the link? Could the giants themselves also rank up there with the Seraphim, who know the love of God, or the Cherubim, who have perfect knowledge of God? As Coffee Talk's Linda Richman says: Talk amongst yourselves.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Aug 14, 2008 12:56 am Given GAINAX's background, FAR = Arisians.

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Thu Aug 14, 2008 1:08 am I looked that up and it seems extremely interesting, I am going to have to do further research on the Arisians. But that is not what this thread is about, it is about what I stated above.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Thu Aug 14, 2008 1:41 am Here are the ranks of the angels in Abrahamic theology: Sachiel: Cherubim Ramiel: Archangel Israfel: ISLAMIC archangel Sandalphon: Archangel Sahaquiel: Archangel (one of the 7 great ones, as well) Iruel: I can find references for him, but not his rank. Probably just angel. Leliel: Same as Iruel I couldn't find references to Shamshel, Gaghiel, Matariel, Bardiel, Zeruel, Arael, Armisael, or Tabris, much to my annoyance. Still, this is interesting, as Sachiel is the only angel of any particular rank. Also of note is the downward trend; as the Angels become more and more intelligent, they descend through the ranks. Quite appropriate, considering the Fruits.

[]:             Quote Post by pat457 » Thu Aug 14, 2008 9:27 am As there is some variance on which Angelic choir a particular (Judeo-Christian-Islamic) Angel belongs to (an individual Angel could be identified with this rank by X or that rank by Y) or even what their names are, this would be quite tricky. Especially as some of the names of the NGE Angels usually do not appear in 'mainstream' sources (I've never yet seen a church of St. Armisael or an angel venerated under the name of Tabris) but are usually from apocryphal works. Here are the three that I know of: SACHIEL: Usually listed as Cherubim (so in Wikipedia), though there is a link here which gives Sachiel as a variant name of an Archangel named Zadkiel (other variants include Tzadkiel, Zadakiel, Zedekiel, or Zedekul). The name Zadkiel , in the above article, was said to have made first appeared in western Patristic literature though I have yet to confirm this. ISRAFEL: One of the four Islamic Archangels, along with Mikhail, Djibril and Izra'il. Although the name "Israfel" does not appear in the Qur'an, mention is made of a trumpet-angel assumed to identify this figure: "And the trumpet shall be blown, so all those that are in the heavens and all those that are in the earth shall swoon, except Allah; then it shall be blown again, then they shall stand up awaiting." —Qur'an (39.68).

Especially as some of the names of the NGE Angels usually do not appear in 'mainstream' sources (I've never yet seen a church of St. Armisael or an angel venerated under the name of Tabris) but are usually from apocryphal works. Here are the three that I know of: SACHIEL: Usually listed as Cherubim (so in Wikipedia), though there is a link here which gives Sachiel as a variant name of an Archangel named Zadkiel (other variants include Tzadkiel, Zadakiel, Zedekiel, or Zedekul). The name Zadkiel , in the above article, was said to have made first appeared in western Patristic literature though I have yet to confirm this. Although the name "Israfel" does not appear in the Qur'an, mention is made of a trumpet-angel assumed to identify this figure: "And the trumpet shall be blown, so all those that are in the heavens and all those that are in the earth shall swoon, except Allah; then it shall be blown again, then they shall stand up awaiting." —Qur'an (39.68).
